bead.title: bench-pr-A2c: per-cell retry with attempt_of / superseded_by for transient errors
bead.description:
PROBLEM: A2b lands the per-cell execution loop but treats every harness/executor failure as terminal. PR A's runner must retry cells that fail with transient errors (connection refused, 5xx, EOF parse), writing each attempt to a fresh cell_dir and back-writing the prior cell with `superseded_by`. This is the one mutation allowed on an otherwise immutable closed cell.

ROOT CAUSE: After A2b, scripts/benchmark/benchmark writes one report.json per (profile,task,rep) and never retries. Transient provider/network errors that should be retried are instead permanently recorded as failures, breaking parity with the Go matrix runner.

PROPOSED FIX (extending scripts/benchmark/benchmark from A2b):
  1. Classify per-cell exit: read result.json error_class. Transient classes: connection_refused, http_5xx, eof_parse, provider_timeout, dial_timeout.
  2. Bounded exponential backoff per cell (max attempts configurable, default 3; base 2s, factor 2). Each retry attempt:
     a. Allocate a fresh cell_dir suffix (e.g. cell_dir.attempt-N) and a new cell_id.
     b. Run the full loop body from A2b into the new cell_dir; write report.json with field `attempt_of: <prior-cell-id>`.
     c. Back-write the prior cell's report.json with `superseded_by: <new-cell-id>` (the sole permitted mutation of a closed cell; preserve all other fields byte-for-byte).
  3. Non-transient errors: no retry, no superseded_by chain, terminal as today.
  4. When max attempts exhausted, the final attempt's report.json carries `final_status:"transient_exhausted"`; chain remains intact via attempt_of/superseded_by links.
  5. Add a tiny test fixture harness `scripts/benchmark/test/fixtures/transient-harness` that fails N times then succeeds, parameterised via env.

NON-SCOPE (preserved from parent):
  - Bash skeleton, listing subcommands, --plan, matrix expansion, preflight, validate (A2a)
  - Per-cell execution loop, report.json composition, sweep.json, resume/--force-rerun/--retry-invalid (A2b)
  - cell-report.schema.json (PR B); tasks_from/task_executor bench-set schema finalization (PR C)
  - Node collector/importers (PR D/E); deleting cmd/bench (PR F); docs (PR G)
  - Concurrency-group flock, --jobs pool, SIGTERM/SIGINT, in-flight.json, budget enforcement, budget.json, budget_halted, go-runner deactivation (A3)
  - Parity fixtures and diff.sh (A4)
  - Harbor image, shell adapters, runtime-probe, agent-leak fix (A1)

GOVERNING: plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Depends on A1, A2a, A2b.

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. TestPerCellRetryWritesAttemptOfAndSupersedes (scripts/benchmark/test/test_runner.sh::test_per_cell_retry_writes_attempt_of_and_supersedes): inject the transient-harness fixture to fail once then succeed; assert the second cell's report.json has `attempt_of:<id1>` and the first cell is back-written with `superseded_by:<id2>` while all other first-cell fields are byte-identical to the pre-retry snapshot. (Parent AC5.)
  2. TestNonTransientErrorIsNotRetried (test_runner.sh::test_non_transient_error_no_retry): inject a fixture that exits with a non-transient error_class; assert exactly one cell exists with no attempt_of/superseded_by fields.
  3. TestTransientExhaustedTerminates (test_runner.sh::test_transient_exhausted_terminates): inject a fixture that always fails transient; assert max-attempts chain is created and the terminal cell has `final_status:"transient_exhausted"`.
  4. TestRetryBackoffIsBounded (test_runner.sh::test_retry_backoff_is_bounded): wall-clock between retry attempt 1 and attempt 2 is ≥ base, between 2 and 3 is ≥ base*factor (within 250ms slack).

GATES: `go test ./...` green (Go unchanged); `lefthook run pre-commit` green; `bash scripts/benchmark/test/run.sh`.
bead.acceptance: All 4 ACs pass; satisfies parent AC5; go test + lefthook gates green.
